name: Under Armour, Inc.

profile.description: Under Armour designs, develops, markets, and distributes athletic and outdoor apparel, footwear, and accessories globally. The company is executing a multi-year brand repositioning under founder-CEO Kevin Plank, who returned in 2024 to lead a restructuring that involves exiting unprofitable channels, reducing inventory, and refocusing on the core performance athletic identity. Under Armour operates in North America, EMEA, Asia-Pacific, and Latin America.

innovation.label: DEVELOPING
innovation.analysis: Under Armour's recent efforts are centered on brand repositioning and channel cleanup rather than genuine product innovation — FY2026 full-year revenue declined 4% to $5.0B with North America down 8% [1][2]. The Connected Fitness platform (MapMyFitness) and UA Connected footwear technology represent digital exploration but lag Nike's 190M+ app ecosystem [5]. No breakthrough product launches have been announced, and the FY2027 outlook projects continued revenue declines [2]. R&D intensity is below Nike and Adidas, and the company holds relevant but declining market share in premium performance athletic wear. Innovation at UA is currently about turnaround execution, not new category creation.

inspiration.label: DEVELOPING
inspiration.analysis: CEO Kevin Plank publicly advocates micromanagement as 'totally underestimated,' employing an 80-20 rule that prioritizes getting the right answer over employee autonomy [4]. Glassdoor rating is 3.7/5 with 73% recommending the company, but career opportunities score only 3.0/5 and senior leadership is a common negative [5]. Multiple restructuring waves and workforce reductions under rotating CEO leadership (Plank to Frisk back to Plank) have eroded organizational stability. The mission 'to make all athletes better through passion, design, and relentless pursuit of innovation' is specific and distinctive, but execution has been inconsistent through five years of contraction [3][5].

infrastructure.label: DEVELOPING
infrastructure.analysis: FY2026 delivered a net loss of $496M ($1.16/share), though adjusted net income was $50M ($0.12/share) [1][2]. Revenue declined 4% to $5.0B with North America down 8%, partially offset by 9% EMEA growth and 9% Latin America growth [1]. D/E of 137% is elevated for an apparel company. ROE is deeply negative at -30%, and profit margin is -10% on a trailing basis. Forward P/E of 20.9x is reasonable only if the turnaround materializes — FY2027 guides to $140-160M adjusted operating income with gross margin expansion of 220-270 bps [2]. Liquidity of $46M daily trading value passes the floor comfortably [6].

ai_resilience.label: NEUTRAL
ai_resilience.analysis: Under Armour's core business — designing, manufacturing, and marketing physical athletic apparel — involves tangible products and brand identity that AI cannot replace [5]. However, competitors with superior data assets (Nike with 190M+ app users, Adidas with advanced supply chain AI) can use AI more effectively for personalization, demand forecasting, and design optimization, creating a competitive gap [5]. Under Armour's VP of Enterprise Data Management oversees AI deployment including generative AI exploration, but the company lacks the scale and data moat of larger rivals [5]. The Connected Fitness platform could benefit from AI-driven personalization but remains subscale. AI Risk Protection: 5 (physical product immune but competitive AI gap). AI Opportunity: 0 (subscale digital assets). Total: 5.

infinite_game.summary: Kevin Plank's return as CEO represents an attempt to reconnect Under Armour with its founding athletic performance identity — a potentially infinite game restoration. However, five years of reactive restructuring, rotating leadership, and brand erosion reveal a company that has been playing defensively rather than building for the long term.

just_cause.analysis: Under Armour's original cause — making athletes better through performance innovation — is genuine, specific, and distinctive, articulated clearly by Plank from the founding era [3]. The return of the founder represents an attempt to restore this cause after years of drift under prior CEO Patrik Frisk. The mission is clear even if recent execution has been inconsistent with five consecutive years of financial deterioration.

trusting_teams.analysis: Multiple restructuring waves, CEO changes (Plank to Frisk back to Plank), and Plank's self-described micromanagement philosophy create an unstable organizational environment [4][5]. Career opportunities score 3.0/5 on Glassdoor — the lowest category — and senior leadership is a recurring negative in employee reviews. The FY2026 net loss of $496M and ongoing transformation indicate financial stress that pressures team morale [1].

worthy_rivals.analysis: Under Armour explicitly positions against Nike and Adidas as worthy rivals whose strengths in brand storytelling, global distribution, and digital ecosystems have exposed UA's weaknesses [3]. Plank's return narrative is explicitly about learning from competitors' advantages. Green Century investors note UA is at a 'competitive disadvantage against rivals such as Nike, Adidas, and Puma' on sustainability [7].

existential_flexibility.analysis: The brand restructuring — exiting unprofitable channels, disposing of non-core assets, accepting revenue decline — represents necessary defensive retrenchment rather than proactive strategic reinvention [1][2]. Under Armour is returning to its roots rather than innovating into new markets. FY2027 guidance of continued revenue decline suggests the transformation timeline extends further [2].

courage_to_lead.analysis: Plank's willingness to accept multi-year revenue contraction (-9.4% FY2025, -4% FY2026, slight decline guided FY2027) in service of rebuilding brand equity demonstrates some long-term thinking over short-term revenue defense [1][2]. However, the retraction of climate commitments under pressure shows limits to courageous leadership on non-financial dimensions [7]. The company retracted its 2021 net-zero pledge, putting it behind Nike, Adidas, and Puma on sustainability credibility.

investment_thesis: Under Armour's bull case is a founder-led turnaround at trough valuation: Kevin Plank's return aims to restore the brand's performance athletic identity, and FY2027 guidance projects gross margin expansion of 220-270 bps with adjusted operating income of $140-160M [2]. International business showed resilience with EMEA +9% and Latin America +9% in FY2026, while the North American channel cleanup positions for higher-quality revenue in subsequent years [1]. At $5.12/share after a multi-year selloff from $25+, UAA trades near tangible book value with significant option value if the turnaround succeeds.
thesis_continued: The bear case is formidable: FY2026 delivered a $496M net loss with revenue declining for the fifth consecutive year to $5.0B, and FY2027 guides to further decline [1][2]. ROE of -30% and D/E of 137% indicate serious financial stress. Nike and Lululemon continue to take share with superior brand execution and digital ecosystems, while UA's retraction of climate commitments creates ESG risk and competitive disadvantage [7]. Plank's micromanagement philosophy and the company's history of leadership instability raise execution concerns for a turnaround that requires 3-5 years of patient brand building [4]. The market is pricing in significant execution risk — appropriately so given the track record. WATCH until North American revenue stabilizes.
key_catalysts: North American revenue stabilization and return to growth in FY2027/2028, demonstrating that channel cleanup and brand repositioning are working after five years of decline [1][2]
FY2027 gross margin expansion of 220-270 bps materializing, validating that exiting low-margin channels improves profitability even as revenue declines [2]

Successful footwear product launch that captures meaningful market share — footwear is UA's weakest segment versus Nike and Adidas, and a breakthrough entry would expand the addressable market significantly [3]
key_risks: Revenue decline extending beyond FY2027, indicating the brand repositioning is failing to resonate with consumers and the turnaround timeline is longer than the balance sheet can support with D/E at 137% [1][2][6]
Nike and Adidas accelerating AI-driven personalization and digital ecosystem advantages, systematically widening the competitive gap while UA's subscale digital platform cannot compete [5]

Kevin Plank leadership instability or departure — the bull case requires founder-led brand restoration over 3-5 years; any disruption restarts the confidence-building clock, and his micromanagement style may limit talent retention [4][5]
sources: Under Armour FY2026 Full-Year Results — Revenue $5.0B (-4%), net loss $496M, May 2026
Under Armour FY2027 Outlook — Revenue slight decline, gross margin +220-270 bps, adj OI $140-160M, May 2026
Under Armour Corporate — Mission, brand strategy, investor relations, about.underarmour.com
Fortune — Kevin Plank micromanagement philosophy, 80-20 rule, September 2025
Glassdoor / Comparably — Under Armour employee reviews, 3.7/5 overall, career opportunities 3.0/5, accessed May 2026
Yahoo Finance — UAA key statistics, D/E 137%, ROE -30%, profit margin -10%, accessed May 2026
Green Century Capital — Under Armour climate commitment retraction, ESG controversy, 2026
